titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.18

p.seg: William, by the grace of God king of the Scots, to all worthy men of his entire land [sends] greeting.

in my presence, and with bishops, barons, and many other worthy men of my realm being present, on the Day of Saint Luke the Evangelist, by the oaths of sworn men, perambulation was made and the limitation of the boundaries fixed between the monks of Melrose and the men of Wedale concerning the pasture of my forest that my ancestors and I gave to the said monks of Melrose in pure and everlasting alms. The aforesaid sworn men indeed swore an oath in front of me and my worthy men, that my forest, which is the pasture of the aforesaid monks of Melrose, extends to the road that leads to the western side of the church of Wedale and as far as the stream which is called Fasseburn.

Wherefore I will, grant and instruct that the said monks peaceably hold and possess the whole pasture aforementioned with all its easements and liberties as far as the boundaries aforementioned in pure, free and everlasting alms, as their own property and their successors', so that none shall have common between them or with them within the aforementioned boundaries of the said pasture, saving to myself and my heirs wild beasts, birds and game within the said forest.
As witnesses Richard bishop of St Andrews, Jocelin bishop of Glasgow, John of Kelso, Osbert abbot of Jedburgh, Earl David, Richard de Morville the constable and many others.
